channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The TikTok ban is being weighed by the Supreme Court. Here’s what to know
item.description: The Supreme Court of the United States is hearing arguments today to decide the fate of TikTok. Lawmakers believe the app, which is owned by the Chinese company ByteDance, poses a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Oracle refuses to yield JavaScript trademark, Deno Land says
item.description: JavaScript runtime provider Deno Land’s efforts to get Oracle to yield the trademark for JavaScript have hit a snag, with Oracle refusing to voluntarily withdraw the trademark, Deno Land said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech unemployment in the US drops to lowest level in more than two years
item.description: Tech hiring rose in December, dropping the IT unemployment rate to 2% — its lowest since November 2023, according to an analysis of the latest jobs data published today by the US Bureau of Labor statistics (BLS). The overall national unemployment rate held steady at 4.1%, according to the BLS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta rolls back diversity and inclusion efforts, appeasing Trump
item.description: Staff will no longer be required to interview candidates from under-represented backgrounds or look to do business with diverse suppliers.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta cutting DEI team, programs in broad shift
item.description: Meta told employees Friday it will cut its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) team and roll back several related programs, marking another major shift in policy for the leading social media company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The L.A. wildfires show how social media has become just another spin room
item.description: It’s hard to remember now, as you scroll through a thicket of porn bots, anti-trans activists, and AI slop, but there was a time when Twitter was credibly labeled our “global town square.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bien-Kahn, Fast Company

item.title: DJI's Mic Mini might just have met its match: the tiny, logo-free Hollyland Lark M2S wireless mic
item.description: Hollyland announces the new Lark M2S wireless mic which is discreet, logo-free and weighs just 7g, set to rival DJI and Rode.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: UAE Investment Firm Pledges $20 Billion for AI-Focused Data Centers
item.description: A UAE investment firm has pledged $20 billion to build new data centers targeting AI across several locations in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Lenovo is building a billion-dollar PC and server plant in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Together with a new headquarters, Lenovo will manufacture “millions” of PCs and servers at a new facility.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AMD blames Ryzen 9800X3D stock shortage on Intel’s ‘horrible’ Arrow Lake launch, rubbing salt in Team Blue’s CPU wounds
item.description: Can’t find a Ryzen 9800X3D CPU for your gaming PC? That’s Intel’s fault, AMD implies, for making Arrow Lake so bad.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amazon S3 Bucket Leaking Sensitive Data for Remote Workers - WebWork Tracker Exposed
item.description: Sensitive screenshots of remote workers are being leaked from an Amazon S3 bucket owned by WebWork Tracker, putting company data and credentials at risk.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: The 10 most in-demand tech jobs for 2025
item.description: As we head into 2025, businesses are dialed in on hiring roles such as software engineer, software developer, and end-user support, as well roles centered around cloud, database management, and systems engineering and administration, according to Robert Half Technology’s 2025 IT salary report.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stretch your AI dollar and accelerate AI adoption
item.description: Given their lack of scale and resources as compared to larger enterprises, it’s no wonder that small to medium businesses (SMBs) weigh every decision to adopt new, emerging technologies more prudently. But this also comes without the layers of bureaucracy that plague larger enterprises.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Data centers are transforming waste heat into community energy assets
item.description: How data centers are turning their biggest liability - excess heat - into valuable community resources.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than 4% PC shipment growth predicted for 2025, but not for what you expect, says IDC
item.description: PC sales certainly weren’t going gangbusters in 2024: They only grew a paltry 1% over 2023. According to new figures from IDC, vendors shipped 262.7 million PCs in 2024. But things did pick up a bit in Q4 2024: Shipments grew 1.8% over the prior year, reaching 68.9 million.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rust 1.84 introduces strict provenance APIs
item.description: Rust 1.84 has been released, bringing strict provenance APIs that can be used in lieu of integer-pointer casts, making the code both easier for developers to reason about and easier for the compiler to analyze, the Rust team said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Zuckerberg met with Trump before ending fact-checking program: GOP senator
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.) said Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg met with President-elect Trump a day ahead of announcing the social media network will eliminate its fact-checking program to prioritize free speech.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Larry Magid: Smart ring tracks health, fitness data with long battery life
item.description: Although no tracker is all encompassing and 100% accurate, the Ringconn seems to give pretty good results
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: The ‘Worst in Show’ CES products put your data at risk and cause waste, privacy advocates say
item.description: So much of the technology showcased at CES includes gadgets made to improve consumers’ lives — whether by leveraging AI to make devices that help people become more efficient, by creating companions to cure loneliness or by providing tools that help people with mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Examining disk space on Linux
item.description: There are many ways to evaluate disk space usage on Linux, and this post examines a number of commands that allow you to view used and available disk space.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Almost nothing remains of Software AG
item.description: Software AG (now legally known as Software GmbH) has sold its Alfabet and Cumulocity divisions, following on from the recent sales of webMethods, StreamSets and TrendMiner.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Facebook to end fact-checking, boost political content
item.description: The company said automated moderation would focus on serious issues like terrorism and child sexual exploitation instead.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: Editor’s note: This is an archive of 2024 incidents as tracked by Cisco subsidiary ThousandEyes. For current trends, see the 2025 outage report and internet health check, which is updated weekly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation
